source_prompt: FigOps의 다음 스펙을 문서화하고 작업 난이도에 따라 팀으로 진행한다. 첫 릴리스는 publication readiness MVP로 축소한다.

activation.reason: The MVP spans a new evidence contract, deterministic reporting, CLI and MCP public surfaces, generated documentation, and independent compatibility verification.

objective: Add an evidence-centered, read-only publication-readiness evaluation that consolidates existing FigOps validation results into blocked, needs_revision, or needs_review without claiming human approval.

assumptions.claim: Existing data-contract, calculation, geometry, preflight, regression, and provenance outputs provide enough evidence for a useful first readiness decision.
assumptions.verification: Inventory live producer schemas and prove every MVP gate maps to an existing field or is reported as unavailable rather than inferred.

assumptions.claim: Unknown or missing required evidence must never be treated as a passing check.
assumptions.verification: Add fixtures for absent, malformed, unsupported-version, and passed=null evidence and assert a blocked result with a stable diagnostic code.

assumptions.claim: A single pure evaluator can serve CLI and MCP without duplicating policy.
assumptions.verification: Contract tests feed the same normalized evidence to both adapters and compare their readiness payloads.

assumptions.claim: The MVP can remain read-only and therefore does not need approval storage, stale-state tracking, or Figure Pack output.
assumptions.verification: Public API and threat-model review confirms that evaluation reads an existing persisted render manifest and writes no report file.

phases.name: Evidence Inventory And Contract Lock
entry_criteria: The MVP scope and repository protocol are available.
exit_criteria: A reviewed contract defines evidence adapters, state precedence, stable finding codes, deterministic serialization, and explicit non-goals.

phases.name: Readiness Domain And Reports
exit_criteria: Normalization, pure evaluation, JSON and Markdown rendering pass deterministic contract tests.

phases.name: CLI And Read-Only MCP Evaluation
entry_criteria: The domain contract is stable and tested.
exit_criteria: CLI and MCP expose the same evaluation semantics while preserving existing commands and trust boundaries.

phases.name: Adversarial Verification And Documentation
entry_criteria: Implementation slices and focused test receipts exist.
exit_criteria: Independent verification cannot refute state precedence, fail-closed evidence handling, deterministic output, compatibility, or scope exclusions.

phases.name: Verified Handoff
entry_criteria: The adversarial verification receipt refutes no acceptance claim.
exit_criteria: The maintainer receives the implementation summary, test evidence, and residual risks without release actions.

workers.role: Deep contract architect for evidence normalization and decision semantics
forbidden_actions: Do not add approval, stale-state, or Figure Pack behavior.
Do not reinterpret missing evidence as success.
Do not alter existing producer schemas in this slice.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: contract receipt with state table, evidence map, stable finding codes, compatibility notes, and unresolved decisions
stop_conditions: a producer ambiguity requires a product decision
scope expansion beyond read-only evaluation is requested

workers.role: Standard implementer for normalized evidence, pure evaluator, and deterministic reports
forbidden_actions: Do not import MCP modules into the domain layer.
Do not include absolute local paths or timestamps in deterministic report content.
Do not add state beyond blocked, needs_revision, and needs_review.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: patch plus focused test receipt covering normalization, evaluation, JSON, and Markdown
stop_conditions: an evidence producer cannot be normalized without changing its public schema
a forbidden state or write path is needed

forbidden_actions: Do not introduce an independent evaluator.
Do not change existing command meanings or exit codes.
Do not write report files; render JSON or Markdown to stdout only.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: CLI patch and test receipt with output examples and exit codes
stop_conditions: CLI manifest evaluation and stdout-format tests pass
a new top-level executable is required

workers.role: Deep MCP read-only surface and trust-boundary implementer
forbidden_actions: Do not require the MCP write-tools flag for evaluation.
Do not add graphhub compatibility aliases.
Do not widen allowed roots or expose absolute paths.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: MCP patch plus schema, handler, redaction, and read-only test receipt
stop_conditions: MCP job_id evaluation works with write tools disabled and uses the existing safe runtime manifest lookup
tool docs and schema generation are synchronized
a trust-boundary widening would be required

forbidden_actions: Do not edit implementation or tests.
Do not accept producer receipts without reproducing key checks.
Do not classify skipped security tests as passing.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: confirmed, refuted, and unverified claims with commands and evidence

artifact_schema.validation_command: python -c "from pathlib import Path; p=Path('publication-readiness-verification.receipt.md'); t=p.read_text(encoding='utf-8').lower(); assert all(k in t for k in ['confirmed','refuted','unverified','commands','residual'])"

parallelism.fan_in_rule: CLI and MCP fan in only after both receipts prove they call the same domain interface and preserve existing compatibility contracts.
barriers: domain determinism gate before public adapters

verification.claim_or_output: State precedence is fail-closed and limited to blocked, needs_revision, and needs_review.
verification.falsifier: Provide missing, malformed, unsupported, passed=null, failed hard-gate, or major-finding evidence and observe needs_review or an undocumented state.
evidence_required: Parameterized state-table tests and golden JSON reports for every precedence branch.

verification.claim_or_output: JSON and Markdown reports are deterministic for identical normalized evidence.
verification.falsifier: Repeat evaluation across key order, path separator, locale, timezone, or CRLF/LF variations and observe semantic or byte drift in deterministic fields.
evidence_required: Cross-variation fixtures, byte comparisons for JSON, and snapshot comparisons for Markdown.

verification.claim_or_output: CLI and MCP use one evaluator and return equivalent readiness semantics.
verification.falsifier: Feed the same evidence through CLI and MCP and observe different state, gate, or finding-code results.
evidence_required: Shared persisted render-manifest fixture exercised through both adapters.

verification.claim_or_output: The MCP evaluation tool is read-only and does not widen trust boundaries.
verification.falsifier: Run with write tools disabled, escaping paths, symlinks, or secrets in evidence and observe a write, root escape, absolute-path disclosure, or secret disclosure.
evidence_required: Read-only job lookup, containment, symlink, and redaction tests with zero security skips in gating CI.

verification.claim_or_output: The MVP contains no approval, stale-state, or Figure Pack behavior.
verification.falsifier: Find public states, commands, MCP tools, storage, or package outputs implementing any excluded capability.
evidence_required: Changed-file review, public-surface inventory, and repository search in the final verification receipt.

risk_gates.trigger: Repository write access outside the files owned by the active slice.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before repository write and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Shell command execution that mutates user data, installs software, or acts outside the repository and isolated test directories.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before shell execution and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Changing existing evidence producer schemas, existing CLI meanings, or existing MCP compatibility aliases.
risk_gates.safe_default: Preserve existing contracts and ask before contract change

risk_gates.trigger: Adding a runtime dependency or changing lock files.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before dependency change and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Widening allowed roots, enabling writes by default, reading secrets, or accessing private external data.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before trust boundary change and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Adding approval, stale-state tracking, Figure Pack generation, or any other excluded feature.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before scope expansion and ask for approval

risk_gates.trigger: Publishing, tagging, deploying, or sending external messages.
risk_gates.safe_default: Stop before external action and ask for approval

invalidators: Invalidate the contract receipt when an evidence producer schema or quality-gate policy changes.
Invalidate domain receipts when normalization, evaluator, report schema, or golden fixtures change.
Invalidate adapter receipts when the domain public interface, CLI parser, MCP registry, or generated tool docs change.
Invalidate final verification when any implementation or test file in the changed-file inventory changes.

first_slice.instruction: Inventory live FigOps evidence producers and write publication-readiness-contract.receipt.json defining the MVP evidence map, state precedence, finding codes, deterministic serialization contract, and zero open decisions without editing implementation files.
first_slice.completion_check: The receipt parses as JSON, maps each required gate to a live evidence producer, defines only three states, and contains an empty open_decisions array.
forbidden_actions: Do not edit implementation files.
Do not invent passing evidence for missing producers.
Do not add approval, stale-state, or Figure Pack scope.

slices.instruction: Inventory current data-contract, calculation, geometry, preflight, regression, and provenance producer schemas with exact source references.
slices.completion_check: Every proposed required gate has a live producer reference or an explicit unavailable classification.
forbidden_actions: Do not edit files.
Do not treat documentation claims as live schema evidence when source disagrees.

slices.instruction: Define and challenge state precedence, stable diagnostic codes, deterministic serialization, path redaction, persisted render-manifest input, safe runtime job lookup, and the read-only threat model.
slices.completion_check: The proposed contract has exactly three states, fails closed on unknown evidence, and contains no excluded capability.
forbidden_actions: Do not edit files.
Do not widen roots or propose write-enabled MCP evaluation.

first_wave.entry_gate: Repository and canonical documentation are readable and no implementation mutation is required for inventory.
first_wave.exit_gate: The merged contract receipt parses, cites live evidence, has zero open decisions, and passes independent scope and threat review.
first_wave.fan_in: The coordinator merges inventory and threat review into one decision-complete contract receipt before unlocking implementation.

waves.entry_gate: wave-0 contract receipt is verified and open_decisions is empty.
waves.exit_gate: Domain contract, state table, fail-closed fixtures, and deterministic report tests pass.

waves.entry_gate: wave-1 domain receipt exists, tests pass, and the public domain interface is stable.
waves.exit_gate: CLI and MCP contract fixtures agree, existing compatibility tests pass, and MCP read-only containment/redaction tests pass.

waves.entry_gate: All implementation receipts, changed-file lists, focused test logs, and generated-doc drift results are available.
waves.exit_gate: Verifier classifies every acceptance claim, refutes none, confirms excluded features are absent, and records full locked QA status.
